content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 26, 1994

Robert E. Jorgensen
321 Highland Avenue
Santa Cruz, CA 95060

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-128

Dear Mr. Jorgensen:

This letter is in response to your request for advice
concerning your duties and responsibilities under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1

QUESTION

As a planning commissioner, may you participate in land use
decisions and general plan amendments which will affect your
employer, the University of California, Santa Cruz?

CONCLUSION

Under the provisions of the Act, you may participate in land
use decisions and general plan amendments which will affect your
employer, the University of California, a public entity.

FACTS

You are the chair of the Planning Commission of the City of
Santa Cruz. You are also employed full-time by the University of
California, Santa Cruz, (the "University") as Assistant Dean of
Social Sciences.

The University is a third party to a land use application
requiring general plan amendments, which is before the planning
commission. The University may receive a gift of more than five
developable acres and utility/infrastructure easements to its
currently landlocked facility, the Long Marine Lab.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-128
Page 2


ANALYSIS

The Act prohibits a public official from making, participat-
ing in making, or in any way attempting to use his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official knows or has reason to know he or she has a financial
interest. (Section 87100.) An official has a financial interest
in a decision if the decision will have a reasonably foreseeable
material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the
public generally, on the official or a member of his or her
immediate family or on:

*****

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

Section 87103(c) and (d).
(Emphasis added.)

You are an Assistant Dean and a paid employee of the
University, a public entity. Salary and related reimbursement for
expenses or per diem received from a state, local or federal
government agency is expressly exempted from the definition of
"income" for purposes of the Act. (Section 82030(b)(2).) Thus,
the University is not considered a "source of income" to you for
purposes of the disclosure or disqualification provisions of the
Act.

In addition, because the University is not an organization or
enterprise operated for profit, it is not a "business entity" as
defined by the Act. (Section 82005.)

Therefore, it appears that the University is not a financial
interest of yours and your status as an employee of the University
will not create a conflict of interest concerning planning
commission decisions affecting the University. Accordingly, you
may participate in the land use decisions and general plan
amendments which may have an effect on the University.

Please note that our advice is limited to the provisions of
the Political Reform Act. We suggest you contact the Office of

File No. A-94-128
Page 3


the Attorney General to ascertain if any other provisions of law
may apply to your situation.

     I trust this answers your question.  Should you have any
further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5901.2


                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: Jim Stecher
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak




_________________________
2 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
